text: Tapering significantly improves time-trial performance in endurance athletes with a moderate effect size.
Tapering significantly improves time to exhaustion performance in endurance athletes with a large effect size.
Tapering does not significantly improve VO2max or economy of movement, suggesting performance gains come from other physiological adaptations.
Tapering combined with a pre-taper overload training block produces significantly greater time-trial performance improvements than conventional tapering alone.
An effective taper should reduce training volume by 41-60% while maintaining training intensity and frequency.
Taper duration of up to 21 days can significantly improve time-trial performance, with effective durations at ≤7, 8-14, and 15-21 day ranges.
Progressive taper (gradual volume reduction) and step taper (abrupt volume reduction) are both effective strategies for improving time-trial performance.
